source_jobs.title: Principal Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ONRAMP

OnRamp transforms B2B customer onboarding into a revenue driver. Our platform automates workflows, streamlines playbooks, and accelerates time-to-value, helping enterprises reduce onboarding time by up to 70%. Backed by leading investors (we just raised our $15M Series A) and trusted by Fortune 15 companies, we’re redefining how companies bring new customers online.

We’ve built the system of record for post-sale workflows, powering onboarding and expansion for enterprise customers including Fortune 15 companies. Now we’re evolving that foundation into something much bigger: an intelligent system that actually does the work.

We’re building an agentic platform that eliminates friction in the B2B customer relationship.


THE ROLE

We're hiring a Principal Product Manager to own the customer-facing side of OnRamp: the portal experience that our customers’ customers live in, and the AI-powered engagement layer that makes every interaction more effective.



This is not a supporting role. You will define what best-in-class end customer engagement looks like, build the roadmap to get there, and ship it. The customer portal is OnRamp's most visible surface — it's where our customers' customers go to complete onboarding, take action, and stay engaged throughout their entire lifecycle. Making that experience exceptional is a direct driver of our customers' retention, expansion, and revenue outcomes.



You'll also be the PM owner of Aero AI's customer-facing agent capabilities: the intelligence embedded in the portal that surfaces contextual guidance, nudges stalled users, answers questions in the moment, and escalates to a human when it matters.

 
 


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

1. Customer Portal Experience Own the end-to-end experience of the OnRamp customer portal — the primary interface for end customers running their onboarding and keeping their customers engaged. Define what a best-in-class portal looks and feels like, identify gaps between where we are and where we need to be, and build a roadmap that closes them.



2. AI-Powered Customer Engagement Own the product direction for Aero AI's portal agent layer. Work closely with engineering and design to build agents that understand where a customer is in their journey, take proactive action to keep them moving, and hand off to humans with the right context when needed. Define the intelligence — the signals, triggers, and interventions — that turn passive portal visits into active engagement.



3. Expanded Engagement Capabilities Identify and define capabilities beyond the current portal that would help our customers drive better onboarding and implementation outcomes. This includes new surfaces, new interaction models, and new ways to close the loop between the customer-facing experience and the internal teams managing it.



4. Cross-Functional Execution Partner with Engineering, Design, Customer Success, and Sales to ship. Translate customer problems and business outcomes into clear, well-scoped specs. Prioritize ruthlessly. Move fast without creating rework.



5. Voice of the Customer Stay close to customers — both our direct customers (the teams using OnRamp to run onboarding) and their end customers (the people going through it). Use that insight to continuously sharpen your roadmap and make the case for what we build next.



 
 


WHO YOU ARE

Customer Experience Obsessed You hold a high bar for what a great end customer experience looks like. You notice friction before customers complain about it, and you know how to translate that instinct into product decisions.



AI-Native Thinker You understand that AI isn't just a feature to bolt on, it's a different way to design products. You've worked with or thought deeply about how AI agents change what's possible in a customer experience, and you're excited to build in that space.



Outcome-Oriented Builder You care about impact, not output. You can connect product decisions to customer outcomes (time-to-value, adopti

source_jobs.title: Devops / Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ONRAMP

OnRamp transforms B2B customer onboarding into a revenue driver. Our platform automates workflows, streamlines playbooks, and accelerates time-to-value, helping enterprises reduce onboarding time by up to 70%. Backed by leading investors (we just raised our $15M Series A) and trusted by Fortune 15 companies, we’re redefining how companies bring new customers online.

We’ve built the system of record for post-sale workflows, powering onboarding and expansion for enterprise customers including Fortune 15 companies. Now we’re evolving that foundation into something much bigger: an intelligent system that actually does the work.

We’re building an agentic platform that eliminates friction in the B2B customer relationship.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring an engineer to own our infrastructure — the AWS platform, deployment systems, and tooling that lets a small, elite team ship at the pace of a much larger one.

This is a high-impact individual contributor role reporting directly to our Head of Engineering. You’ll have end-to-end ownership of how our software is built, deployed, secured, and operated — including the infrastructure behind our AI agents. And like everyone on this team, you’ll use AI itself as a core tool of the trade: agents and LLM tooling for solving infrastructure problems, not just supporting them.

We’re deliberately not posting a checklist of certifications and years of experience. We care about what you can build and run, not what your last title was — we’ll calibrate level, scope, and compensation to you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own our AWS infrastructure end to end: architecture, infrastructure-as-code, networking, cost, and scale

 - Build the CI/CD and deployment systems that let engineers ship to production many times a day with confidence

 - Attack DevOps problems with AI: use coding agents and LLM tooling to write and refactor infrastructure code, debug incidents, automate runbooks, analyze logs, and systematically eliminate toil

 - Build the infrastructure behind our AI: agent execution environments, LLM observability, evaluation pipelines, and the plumbing that makes agentic features reliable in production

 - Own observability, reliability, and incident response — enterprise customers depend on us, and you’ll keep the platform worthy of that trust

 - Strengthen our security and compliance posture (we’re SOC 2 and HIPAA compliant) as we move upmarket

 - Multiply the team: build paved roads, automation, and AI-powered tooling that make every engineer here faster




WHO YOU ARE

 - Deep, hands-on AWS experience — you’ve designed, built, and operated production infrastructure that real businesses depend on

 - Fluent in infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar) and modern deployment practices: containers, CI/CD, zero-downtime releases

 - You already build with AI. Coding agents and LLM tools are part of how you write IaC, investigate incidents, and automate operations — and you’re constantly finding new ways to apply them

 - You reach for AI before you reach for toil: when you see a recurring problem, your instinct is to build an agent or automation that solves it permanently

 - A security mindset by default — you’ve supported or driven compliance efforts like SOC 2 or HIPAA, or you’re ready to own that surface

 - A builder at heart — comfortable writing real software, not just configuration

 - High ownership and pragmatic — you measure yourself on the team’s velocity and the platform’s reliability, not the number of fires fought




WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE

 - Greenfield ownership: define the platform for an AI-first company at exactly the stage where your decisions compound

 - Infrastructure for agents is a new discipline — you’ll be inventing practices, not following a runbook

 - Your leverage is the whole team’s velocity: on a lean team, great platform work is visible immediately




LOCATION & HOW WE WORK

We’re a Boston-centered engineering culture — our home base i

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer (AI-Native)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ONRAMP

OnRamp transforms B2B customer onboarding into a revenue driver. Our platform automates workflows, streamlines playbooks, and accelerates time-to-value, helping enterprises reduce onboarding time by up to 70%. Backed by leading investors (we just raised our $15M Series A) and trusted by Fortune 15 companies, we’re redefining how companies bring new customers online.

We’ve built the system of record for post-sale workflows, powering onboarding and expansion for enterprise customers including Fortune 15 companies. Now we’re evolving that foundation into something much bigger: an intelligent system that actually does the work.

We’re building an agentic platform that eliminates friction in the B2B customer relationship.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a software engineer who has figured out what most engineers are still catching up to: how to use AI to build enterprise-grade software dramatically faster — without sacrificing quality.

To be clear about what this is and isn’t: this is not an ML research role, and it’s not a prompt-engineering role. It’s a software engineering role for someone with real fundamentals who has made AI a core part of how they build — someone who directs coding agents, runs parallel workstreams, and ships in a week what used to take a team a quarter.

This is a high-impact individual contributor role on a small, elite team, reporting directly to our Head of Engineering. Our whole engineering organization is being built around AI leverage — you won’t be the odd one out fighting for tooling budget. You’ll be a founding example of how we work.

We’re deliberately not posting a checklist of frameworks and years of experience. We care about what you can build and how fast you learn, not what your last title was — we’ll calibrate level, scope, and compensation to you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Ship enterprise-grade features end to end — from ambiguous product problem to production — at a pace that surprises people, with AI in the loop at every step

 - Direct coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or whatever wins next) as a force multiplier: parallel workstreams, asynchronous agents finishing work while you sleep, and your judgment steering all of it

 - Own the quality bar that makes speed sustainable: architecture, code review, testing, and guardrails that keep AI-generated code enterprise-ready — our customers include Fortune 15 companies

 - Work across the full stack of a modern web platform on AWS, including the agentic AI features inside our product

 - Help write the playbook for AI-augmented development at OnRamp — the workflows, tooling, and standards that make every engineer here faster

 - Partner tightly with Product and talk directly with customers, so what you build maps to real value




WHO YOU ARE

 - A software engineer first. You have strong fundamentals — system design, data modeling, APIs, debugging, testing — because AI amplifies judgment; it doesn’t replace it

 - You’ve fully integrated AI into how you build. Not autocomplete — agents doing real, delegated work. You can describe your workflow in detail and it’s clearly ahead of the curve

 - You have proof. You can point to production software you shipped with AI leverage — solo output that used to require a team — and explain exactly how you did it

 - You know where AI fails. You’ve developed the review discipline, testing habits, and skepticism to catch what agents get wrong before customers do

 - Enterprise-minded: you understand that reliability, security, and data integrity are features, and you build like people’s businesses depend on your code — because they will

 - High ownership and product-minded — you run toward ambiguity, make calls, and measure yourself on outcomes, not tickets




WHAT MAKES THIS ROLE UNIQUE

 - The entire company is built around this way of working — AI leverage is the engineering strategy, not a side experiment

 - A lean team shipping at the impact of a much larger organization, on a re

notes: Recent Series A funding, high-growth product company with strong engineering presence
